functions.name: backports.tarfile.stn
documentation: Convert a string to a null-terminated bytes object.
Some useful functions

functions.name: backports.tarfile.nts
documentation: Convert a null-terminated bytes object to a string.

functions.name: backports.tarfile.nti
documentation: Convert a number field to a python number.

functions.name: backports.tarfile.itn
documentation: Convert a python number to a number field.

functions.name: backports.tarfile.calc_chksums
documentation:
Calculate the checksum for a member's header by summing up all
characters except for the chksum field which is treated as if
it was filled with spaces. According to the GNU tar sources,
some tars (Sun and NeXT) calculate chksum with signed char,
which will be different if there are chars in the buffer with
the high bit set. So we calculate two checksums, unsigned and
signed.

functions.name: backports.tarfile.copyfileobj
documentation:
Copy length bytes from fileobj src to fileobj dst.
If length is None, copy the entire content.

functions.name: backports.tarfile.is_tarfile
documentation:
Return True if name points to a tar archive that we
are able to handle, else return False.

'name' should be a string, file, or file-like object.
exported functions

Set the stream's file pointer to pos. Negative seeking

documentation: Return the next size number of bytes from the stream.

methods.name: backports.tarfile._Stream._read
documentation: Return size bytes from the stream.

methods.name: backports.tarfile._Stream.__read
documentation:
Return size bytes from stream. If internal buffer is empty,
read another block from the stream.

Class that serves as an adapter between TarFile and
a stream-like object.  The stream-like object only
needs to have a read() or write() method that works with bytes,
and the method is accessed blockwise.
Use of gzip or bzip2 compression is possible.
A stream-like object could be for example: sys.stdin.buffer,
sys.stdout.buffer, a socket, a tape device etc.

_Stream is intended to be used only internally.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarInfo.__init__
documentation:
Construct a TarInfo object. name is the optional name
of the member.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarInfo.replace
documentation: Return a deep copy of self with the given attributes replaced.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarInfo.get_info
documentation: Return the TarInfo's attributes as a dictionary.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarInfo.tobuf
documentation: Return a tar header as a string of 512 byte blocks.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarInfo.create_ustar_header
documentation: Return the object as a ustar header block.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarInfo.create_gnu_header
documentation: Return the object as a GNU header block sequence.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarInfo.create_pax_header
documentation:
Return the object as a ustar header block. If it cannot be
represented this way, prepend a pax extended header sequence
with supplement information.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarInfo.create_pax_global_header
documentation: Return the object as a pax global header block sequence.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarInfo._posix_split_name
documentation:
Split a name longer than 100 chars into a prefix
and a name part.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarInfo._create_header
documentation:
Return a header block. info is a dictionary with file
information, format must be one of the *_FORMAT constants.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarInfo._create_payload
documentation:
Return the string payload filled with zero bytes
up to the next 512 byte border.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarInfo._create_gnu_long_header
documentation:
Return a GNUTYPE_LONGNAME or GNUTYPE_LONGLINK sequence
for name.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarInfo._create_pax_generic_header
documentation:
Return a POSIX.1-2008 extended or global header sequence
that contains a list of keyword, value pairs. The values
must be strings.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarInfo.frombuf
documentation: Construct a TarInfo object from a 512 byte bytes object.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarInfo.fromtarfile
documentation:
Return the next TarInfo object from TarFile object
tarfile.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarInfo._proc_member
documentation:
Choose the right processing method depending on
the type and call it.
The following are methods that are called depending on the type of a
member. The entry point is _proc_member() which can be overridden in a
subclass to add custom _proc_*() methods. A _proc_*() method MUST
implement the following
operations:
1. Set self.offset_data to the position where the data blocks begin,
if there is data that follows.
2. Set tarfile.offset to the position where the next member's header will
begin.
3. Return self or another valid TarInfo object.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarInfo._proc_builtin
documentation:
Process a builtin type or an unknown type which
will be treated as a regular file.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarInfo._proc_gnulong
documentation:
Process the blocks that hold a GNU longname
or longlink member.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarInfo._proc_sparse
documentation: Process a GNU sparse header plus extra headers.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarInfo._proc_pax
documentation:
Process an extended or global header as described in
POSIX.1-2008.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarInfo._proc_gnusparse_00
documentation: Process a GNU tar extended sparse header, version 0.0.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarInfo._proc_gnusparse_01
documentation: Process a GNU tar extended sparse header, version 0.1.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarInfo._proc_gnusparse_10
documentation: Process a GNU tar extended sparse header, version 1.0.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarInfo._apply_pax_info
documentation:
Replace fields with supplemental information from a previous
pax extended or global header.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarInfo._decode_pax_field
documentation: Decode a single field from a pax record.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarInfo._block
documentation:
Round up a byte count by BLOCKSIZE and return it,
e.g. _block(834) => 1024.

Informational class which holds the details about an
archive member given by a tar header block.
TarInfo objects are returned by TarFile.getmember(),
TarFile.getmembers() and TarFile.gettarinfo() and are
usually created internally.

classes.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.__init__
documentation:
Open an (uncompressed) tar archive 'name'. 'mode' is either 'r' to
read from an existing archive, 'a' to append data to an existing
file or 'w' to create a new file overwriting an existing one. 'mode'
defaults to 'r'.
If 'fileobj' is given, it is used for reading or writing data. If it
can be determined, 'mode' is overridden by 'fileobj's mode.
'fileobj' is not closed, when TarFile is closed.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.open
documentation:
Open a tar archive for reading, writing or appending. Return
an appropriate TarFile class.

mode:
'r' or 'r:\*' open for reading with transparent compression
'r:'         open for reading exclusively uncompressed
'r:gz'       open for reading with gzip compression
'r:bz2'      open for reading with bzip2 compression
'r:xz'       open for reading with lzma compression
'a' or 'a:'  open for appending, creating the file if necessary
'w' or 'w:'  open for writing without compression
'w:gz'       open for writing with gzip compression
'w:bz2'      open for writing with bzip2 compression
'w:xz'       open for writing with lzma compression

'x' or 'x:'  create a tarfile exclusively without compression, raise
             an exception if the file is already created
'x:gz'       create a gzip compressed tarfile, raise an exception
             if the file is already created
'x:bz2'      create a bzip2 compressed tarfile, raise an exception
             if the file is already created
'x:xz'       create an lzma compressed tarfile, raise an exception
             if the file is already created

'r|\*'        open a stream of tar blocks with transparent compression
'r|'         open an uncompressed stream of tar blocks for reading
'r|gz'       open a gzip compressed stream of tar blocks
'r|bz2'      open a bzip2 compressed stream of tar blocks
'r|xz'       open an lzma compressed stream of tar blocks
'w|'         open an uncompressed stream for writing
'w|gz'       open a gzip compressed stream for writing
'w|bz2'      open a bzip2 compressed stream for writing
'w|xz'       open an lzma compressed stream for writing
Below are the classmethods which act as alternate constructors to the
TarFile class. The open() method is the only one that is needed for
public use; it is the "super"-constructor and is able to select an
adequate "sub"-constructor for a particular compression using the mapping
from OPEN_METH.
This concept allows one to subclass TarFile without losing the comfort of
the super-constructor. A sub-constructor is registered and made available
by adding it to the mapping in OPEN_METH.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.taropen
documentation: Open uncompressed tar archive name for reading or writing.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.gzopen
documentation:
Open gzip compressed tar archive name for reading or writing.
Appending is not allowed.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.bz2open
documentation:
Open bzip2 compressed tar archive name for reading or writing.
Appending is not allowed.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.xzopen
documentation:
Open lzma compressed tar archive name for reading or writing.
Appending is not allowed.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.close
documentation:
Close the TarFile. In write-mode, two finishing zero blocks are
appended to the archive.
The public methods which TarFile provides:

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.getmember
documentation:
Return a TarInfo object for member 'name'. If 'name' can not be
found in the archive, KeyError is raised. If a member occurs more
than once in the archive, its last occurrence is assumed to be the
most up-to-date version.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.getmembers
documentation:
Return the members of the archive as a list of TarInfo objects. The
list has the same order as the members in the archive.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.getnames
documentation:
Return the members of the archive as a list of their names. It has
the same order as the list returned by getmembers().

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.gettarinfo
documentation:
Create a TarInfo object from the result of os.stat or equivalent
on an existing file. The file is either named by 'name', or
specified as a file object 'fileobj' with a file descriptor. If
given, 'arcname' specifies an alternative name for the file in the
archive, otherwise, the name is taken from the 'name' attribute of
'fileobj', or the 'name' argument. The name should be a text
string.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.list
documentation:
Print a table of contents to sys.stdout. If 'verbose' is False, only
the names of the members are printed. If it is True, an 'ls -l'-like
output is produced. 'members' is optional and must be a subset of the
list returned by getmembers().

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.add
documentation:
Add the file 'name' to the archive. 'name' may be any type of file
(directory, fifo, symbolic link, etc.). If given, 'arcname'
specifies an alternative name for the file in the archive.
Directories are added recursively by default. This can be avoided by
setting 'recursive' to False. 'filter' is a function
that expects a TarInfo object argument and returns the changed
TarInfo object, if it returns None the TarInfo object will be
excluded from the archive.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.addfile
documentation:
Add the TarInfo object 'tarinfo' to the archive. If 'tarinfo' represents
a non zero-size regular file, the 'fileobj' argument should be a binary file,
and tarinfo.size bytes are read from it and added to the archive.
You can create TarInfo objects directly, or by using gettarinfo().

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.extractall
documentation:
Extract all members from the archive to the current working
directory and set owner, modification time and permissions on
directories afterwards. 'path' specifies a different directory
to extract to. 'members' is optional and must be a subset of the
list returned by getmembers(). If 'numeric_owner' is True, only
the numbers for user/group names are used and not the names.

The 'filter' function will be called on each member just
before extraction.
It can return a changed TarInfo or None to skip the member.
String names of common filters are accepted.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.extract
documentation:
Extract a member from the archive to the current working directory,
using its full name. Its file information is extracted as accurately
as possible. 'member' may be a filename or a TarInfo object. You can
specify a different directory using 'path'. File attributes (owner,
mtime, mode) are set unless 'set_attrs' is False. If 'numeric_owner'
is True, only the numbers for user/group names are used and not
the names.

The 'filter' function will be called before extraction.
It can return a changed TarInfo or None to skip the member.
String names of common filters are accepted.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile._get_extract_tarinfo
documentation: Get filtered TarInfo (or None) from member, which might be a str

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile._extract_one
documentation: Extract from filtered tarinfo to disk

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile._handle_nonfatal_error
documentation: Handle non-fatal error (ExtractError) according to errorlevel

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile._handle_fatal_error
documentation: Handle "fatal" error according to self.errorlevel

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.extractfile
documentation:
Extract a member from the archive as a file object. 'member' may be
a filename or a TarInfo object. If 'member' is a regular file or
a link, an io.BufferedReader object is returned. For all other
existing members, None is returned. If 'member' does not appear
in the archive, KeyError is raised.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile._extract_member
documentation:
Extract the TarInfo object tarinfo to a physical
file called targetpath.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.makedir
documentation: Make a directory called targetpath.
Below are the different file methods. They are called via
_extract_member() when extract() is called. They can be replaced in a
subclass to implement other functionality.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.makefile
documentation: Make a file called targetpath.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.makeunknown
documentation:
Make a file from a TarInfo object with an unknown type
at targetpath.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.makefifo
documentation: Make a fifo called targetpath.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.makedev
documentation: Make a character or block device called targetpath.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.makelink
documentation:
Make a (symbolic) link called targetpath. If it cannot be created
(platform limitation), we try to make a copy of the referenced file
instead of a link.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.chown
documentation:
Set owner of targetpath according to tarinfo. If numeric_owner
is True, use .gid/.uid instead of .gname/.uname. If numeric_owner
is False, fall back to .gid/.uid when the search based on name
fails.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.chmod
documentation: Set file permissions of targetpath according to tarinfo.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.utime
documentation: Set modification time of targetpath according to tarinfo.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.next
documentation:
Return the next member of the archive as a TarInfo object, when
TarFile is opened for reading. Return None if there is no more
available.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile._getmember
documentation:
Find an archive member by name from bottom to top.
If tarinfo is given, it is used as the starting point.
Little helper methods:

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile._load
documentation:
Read through the entire archive file and look for readable
members. This should not run if the file is set to stream.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile._check
documentation:
Check if TarFile is still open, and if the operation's mode
corresponds to TarFile's mode.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile._find_link_target
documentation:
Find the target member of a symlink or hardlink member in the
archive.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.__iter__
documentation: Provide an iterator object.

methods.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile._dbg
documentation: Write debugging output to sys.stderr.

class_variables.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.debug
documentation: May be set from 0 (no msgs) to 3 (all msgs)

class_variables.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.dereference
documentation: If true, add content of linked file to the

class_variables.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.ignore_zeros
documentation: If true, skips empty or invalid blocks and

class_variables.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.errorlevel
documentation: If 0, fatal errors only appear in debug

class_variables.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.format
documentation: The format to use when creating an archive.

class_variables.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.encoding
documentation: Encoding for 8-bit character strings.

class_variables.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.errors
documentation: Error handler for unicode conversion.

class_variables.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.tarinfo
documentation: The default TarInfo class to use.

class_variables.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.fileobject
documentation: The file-object for extractfile().

class_variables.name: backports.tarfile.TarFile.extraction_filter
documentation: The default filter for extraction.
The TarFile Class provides an interface to tar archives.
class TarInfo
